element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Player zoning and basic city layout
Quality of life and population health

element_introduction_order.element: Disaster events (floods, tornadoes, fires, earthquakes, monsters)

rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Asymmetric and player-driven; no real-time pressure in sandbox mode. Time-limited scenarios create acute tension windows. Disasters provide unpredictable disruption spikes.

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Service buildings (hospitals, churches) placement is optional; reward buildings unlock as achievements

difficulty_curve.early_game: Player learns zone mechanics and basic infrastructure with no mandatory pressure in sandbox mode
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Managing traffic, power distribution, and economic balance. Disasters may begin occurring
difficulty_curve.late_game: Optimization for large populations, balancing competing needs, managing complex interdependencies. Disasters escalate in frequency and severity
notable_spikes: Budget crisis from poor tax/spend balance

notable_level_patterns.description: Open-ended, non-linear progression. Player sets own goals. No fail state unless choosing scenario mode.
Goal-oriented, time-limited objectives based on real or fictional cities. Win/loss conditions imposed. Suggested by publisher to add structure.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Historical city scenarios (e.g., real-world city layouts)

notable_level_patterns.description: Unpredictable crisis events break stability rhythm and force reactive management. Range from floods to monster attacks.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Any gameplay affected by random disaster trigger
